_**User**_

Write the file `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/docs/accessibility.md` using the Write tool.

This is the accessibility design document for BaseSkill — a free, open-source, offline-first educational PWA for children (Pre-K through Year 6+). The app must comply with WCAG 2.1 AA.

The document must be comprehensive, specific, and actionable for AI agents implementing the app. Cover:

1. **Overview and Commitment**:
   - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance target
   - Accessibility is a core feature, not an afterthought
   - Key populations: pre-readers (rely on TTS), motor-impaired children (pointer limitations), colorblind users, screen reader users
   - Testing: axe-core in component tests + Playwright accessibility audits

2. **WCAG 2.1 AA Checklist** — Organize by POUR principles. For each applicable criterion, note the level (A or AA), a brief description, and BaseSkill implementation note:

   **Perceivable:**
   - 1.1.1 Non-text Content (A) — all images have meaningful alt text; decorative images have `alt=""`; game illustrations described for screen readers
   - 1.2.x Audio/Video — not applicable (no audio/video content; TTS is generated speech)
   - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (A) — semantic HTML; tables for data; headings in logical order; lists for list content
   - 1.3.3 Sensory Characteristics (A) — instructions never rely on shape/color alone ("the red button"); use text labels
   - 1.4.1 Use of Color (A) — color never sole differentiator in games; shape + label always accompany color
   - 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) (AA) — 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text; verified in all 6 themes
   - 1.4.4 Resize Text (AA) — text scales to 200% without loss of content; em/rem units throughout
   - 1.4.10 Reflow (AA) — content reflows at 320px width; no horizontal scroll except game canvas
   - 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast (AA) — UI components (buttons, inputs, drag zones) have 3:1 contrast against background
   - 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus (AA) — tooltips dismissable, persistent on hover

   **Operable:**
   - 2.1.1 Keyboard (A) — all interactive elements keyboard-accessible; games have keyboard alternatives
   - 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap (A) — modals/dialogs have focus trap with Escape to close
   - 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks (A) — skip-to-main-content link at page top
   - 2.4.3 Focus Order (A) — logical tab order matches visual order
   - 2.4.4 Link Purpose (A) — link text describes destination; no "click here"
   - 2.4.6 Headings and Labels (AA) — descriptive heading text; form inputs have associated labels
   - 2.4.7 Focus Visible (AA) — custom focus indicator: 3px solid `--color-accent` with 2px offset
   - 2.5.3 Label in Name (A) — visible label text is contained in accessible name
   - 2.5.5 Target Size (AA) — minimum 44x44px (CSS), recommended 48x48px, Pre-K/K targets 64x64px

   **Understandable:**
   - 3.1.1 Language of Page (A) — `<html lang="en">` (or active language code) set dynamically on profile language change
   - 3.1.2 Language of Parts (AA) — `lang` attribute on content that differs from page language
   - 3.2.1 On Focus (A) — focusing an element does not trigger unexpected context changes
   - 3.2.2 On Input (A) — changing a setting doesn't auto-submit or navigate without warning
   - 3.3.1 Error Identification (A) — errors identified in text (not just color); associated with field
   - 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions (A) — form inputs have descriptive labels; complex inputs have instructions

   **Robust:**
   - 4.1.1 Parsing (A) — valid HTML; no duplicate IDs; proper nesting
   - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (A) — all custom interactive elements have ARIA name, role, state
   - 4.1.3 Status Messages (AA) — status messages (offline, sync complete, score) announced via `aria-live`

3. **Screen Reader Support**:
   - **ARIA live regions**: `aria-live="polite"` for game state changes (score, encouragement), `aria-live="assertive"` for critical alerts (offline, error)
   - **ARIA roles for game elements**: drag items get `role="button"` + `aria-grabbed`, drop zones get `role="region"` + `aria-dropeffect`
   - **Focus management**: on modal open, focus moves to modal first focusable element; on modal close, focus returns to trigger element; on game start, focus moves to game area
   - **Game state announcements**: current question number, score, timer status announced via live region
   - **LetterTracer**: canvas has `role="img"` + descriptive `aria-label`; keyboard alternative: tap-to-place mode activated by Enter key

4. **Colorblind Friendliness**:
   - All 6 pre-defined themes use colorblind-safe palettes (tested against deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia simulations)
   - Games never use color as the only differentiator:
     - Number Match: numbers labeled as text, not just colored differently
     - Word Builder: letter tiles differentiated by letter character + position, not color
     - Math Facts: answer choices differentiated by position + number text, not color
   - Progress indicators: use icons (stars, checkmarks) not just green/red
   - Error states: icon + text, not just red color

5. **Motor Accessibility**:
   - **Large touch targets**: minimum 48x48px CSS (not just hit area). Pre-K/K: 64px. No dense menus.
   - **Keyboard navigation for all games**:
     - DragAndDrop: items selectable with Space, moved to zones with arrow keys, dropped with Enter
     - MultipleChoice: arrow key navigation between options, Enter to select
     - LetterTracer: Enter to switch to tap-to-place mode; Tab between placement points
     - Timer: can be paused with P or Space (if enabled)
   - **Tap-to-place alternative for LetterTracer**: child taps points along the letter outline instead of drawing; dots appear at tap locations
   - **Magnetic snap for DragAndDrop**: 60px snap threshold assists children with limited motor precision
   - **No time pressure** unless enabled: timer is off by default; parent enables if desired
   - **Pointer Events API**: single unified handler for mouse, touch, and stylus

6. **Cognitive Accessibility**:
   - **Simple language**: instructions use grade-appropriate vocabulary; short sentences
   - **TTS for all instructions**: no pre-reader needs to read to navigate or play
   - **Consistent layouts**: same UI patterns across all games (back button top-left, score top-right, game area center)
   - **Undo/retry always available**: children can always try again; no dead ends
   - **Visual progress**: progress bar and star count always visible during gameplay
   - **Encouraging tone**: all feedback text is positive; no "Wrong!" or "Incorrect!" — use "Try again!" or "Almost!"
   - **Reduced cognitive load**: one task per screen; no simultaneous multiple choices requiring reading + listening

7. **Speech API Fallbacks**:
   When Web Speech API (TTS or STT) is unavailable or denied:
   - **TTS fallback**: instructions displayed as visible text. For Pre-K/K games requiring TTS, a "Read to me" button appears for parent assistance.
   - **STT fallback (Read Aloud game)**: text input field appears. Child can type the sentence. Grade-appropriate keyboard or on-screen keyboard hint.
   - **Fallback detection**: check `'speechSynthesis' in window` and `'SpeechRecognition' in window || 'webkitSpeechRecognition' in window`
   - **Permission denied**: show friendly message "Please allow microphone access to use voice features" with icon-based alternative

8. **Testing and Verification**:
   - axe-core in every component test via `@axe-core/react` or `jest-axe` — zero violations policy
   - Playwright `checkAccessibility()` on all page routes (using `@axe-core/playwright`)
   - Manual screen reader testing: VoiceOver (iOS/macOS), TalkBack (Android), NVDA (Windows)
   - Color contrast verification: all themes verified with Colour Contrast Analyser
   - Keyboard navigation smoke test in Playwright: Tab through all interactive elements on key pages

After writing the file, return "DONE: docs/accessibility.md written successfully".
